content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 29,
1992
Honorable Alfred M.
Damian
City of Tehachapi
City Hall
P.O.
Box 668
Tehachapi,
CA
95361
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-92—454
Dear Councilmember Damian:
This is in regard to your letter requesting assistance
concerning the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act
(the “Act).-/
We are returning your letter without response.
Regulation
18329(c)
which sets out the Fair Political Practices Commission’s
jurisdiction with respect to the issuance of advice letters sets
forth the circumstances under which the Commission will decline to
provide formal or informal advice.
Formal or informal assistance may be requested only by a
person whose duties under the Act are in question or that person’s
authorized representative.
Moreover,
the Commission will provide
informal advice to a person with a duty to advise other persons
relating to their duties or actions under the Act.
Informal
assistance may also be requested by any agency whose members or
employees are subject to the provisions of the Act.
We are however enclosing general information relating to the
questions you have posed.
Hopefully, this will assist you.
If
you believe there has been
a violation of the provisions of the
Act,
you may wish to file a complaint with our Enforcement
Division.
They may be reached at
(916)
322—6441.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Advice File No.
1—92—454
Page Two
If you have any questions regarding this policy, please feel
free to contact the Commission at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Actng General Counsel
/7
L
Jeanette E.
Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division
SH/JET/ jt
Enclosures

Regulation 18329(c) which sets out the Fair Political Practices Commission’s jurisdiction with respect to the issuance of advice letters sets forth the circumstances under which the Commission will decline to provide formal or informal advice. Formal or informal assistance may be requested only by a person whose duties under the Act are in question or that person’s authorized representative. Moreover, the Commission will provide informal advice to a person with a duty to advise other persons relating to their duties or actions under the Act. Informal assistance may also be requested by any agency whose members or employees are subject to the provisions of the Act. We are however enclosing general information relating to the questions you have posed. Hopefully, this will assist you.
